questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. She was pessimistic about her exam results, convinced she had performed poorly.
questions.explanation: D) Pessimistic(adjective) – negative, gloomy, hopeless, doubtful, cynical निराशावादी Antonym: Optimistic(adjective) – Hopeful, positive, confident (आशावादी) Gloomy(adjective) – Dark, depressing, sad (उदास) Cynical(adjective) – Distrustful, skeptical (संदेहवादी) Doubtful(adjective) – Uncertain, unsure (संदिग्ध)

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence. The lack of resources was a thing that prevented us from completing the project on time.
questions.explanation: A)Bar(noun) – A circumstance or obstacle that prevents or hinders progress. रोक

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed select ‘No improvement required’. He fell over in studies owing to his illness.
questions.explanation: D) fell over'के बदले 'fell behind' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँ पर "owing to his illness" के वजह से पढ़ाई में पिछड़ जाने की बात की जा रही है, इसलिए 'fell behind' सही है। fell behind' will be used instead of 'fell over' because here it refers to lagging in studies due to his illness, hence 'fell behind' is appropriate.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. The new policy aims to stimulate economic growth by encouraging investments.
questions.explanation: C) Stimulate(verb) – boost, encourage, spur, energize, trigger प्रोत्साहित करना Antonym: Discourage(verb) – Dishearten, demotivate, deter (हतोत्साहित करना) Boost(verb) – Increase, enhance (बढ़ावा देना) Encourage(verb) – Motivate, inspire (प्रोत्साहित करना) Trigger(verb) – Activate, initiate (शुरू करना)

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that gives their correct logical sequence. A) Which means that in the years ahead, one out of every five workers deployed globally could be an Indian. B) The world is indeed looking up to the Indian economy as a ‘bright star’, as the Finance Minister noted in the Budget speech on February 1. C) In 2020, India accounted for 20.6% of the worldwide population of 15 to 29 year olds. D) No doubt, the rest of the world foresees a fortune in India’s young population. E) But are our policymakers doing enough to realise the possibilities that are unfolding?
questions.explanation: C)BCADE B:It sets the context by referencing an authoritative figure (the Finance Minister) and mentioning a significant event (Budget speech on February 1). C:The time sequence is a major clue here. The Budget speech mentioned in B happened in a year after 2020 (probably in 2021 since February 1 is mentioned). Sentence C offers statistical evidence from 2020 about India's young population, which is a key basis for the points made in subsequent sentencesA:Sentence A starts with the connector "Which means", signifying it is drawing a conclusion or implication from a previously given data or statement.D:The sentence D supports and expands upon the conclusion made in A.E:The connector "But" indicates a shift or contrast from the previously optimistic tone, raising a question about whether India is leveraging this demographic advantage effectively. After making points about India's potential, E naturally brings a questioning or reflective angle, prompting the reader to think about policy and its effectiveness.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. Cuba is known as the sugar bowl of the world. It is part of the Caribbean _____________. This country _______________ a lot of sugar in the decade of 1960s.
questions.explanation: B)Region'का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है "एक बड़ा भौगोलिक क्षेत्र," और यह sentence में 'Caribbean' के संदर्भ में सबसे उपयुक्त है। जबकि 'area' का अर्थ है "सामान्य क्षेत्र," यह यहां contextually कम सही है। 'Stretch' का अर्थ है "फैलाव," लेकिन इसे भौगोलिक संदर्भ में आमतौर पर इस तरह से इस्तेमाल नहीं किया जाता। 'Sector' का अर्थ है "विभाग" या "खंड," जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं बैठता।'Produced'का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है "उत्पादन करना," और वाक्य में 'a lot of sugar in the decade of 1960s' का उल्लेख किया गया है, जो 'produced' के अर्थ के साथ मेल खाता है। 'Cropped' का अर्थ है "फसल लगाना," जो यहां उचित नहीं है। 'Processed' का अर्थ है "प्रसंस्करण करना," लेकिन यह संदर्भ में गलत है क्योंकि वाक्य 'उत्पादन' पर जोर देता है। 'Created' का अर्थ है "निर्माण करना," लेकिन यह चीनी उत्पादन के लिए use नहीं किया जाता। 'Region'is the correct word as it means "a large geographic area," which fits the context of 'Caribbean.' While 'area' refers to a general location, it is less specific in this context. 'Stretch' implies "a span," but it is not commonly used in a geographical sense like this. 'Sector' refers to a "division" or "segment," which is not appropriate here. 'Produced' is correct as it means "to produce," aligning with the mention of "a lot of sugar in the decade of 1960s." 'Cropped' refers to "cultivation," which is not fitting here. 'Processed' means "to refine or treat," but the context emphasizes production. 'Created' means "to create," which does not apply to sugar production.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. Scientists are working to harness solar energy more efficiently to combat climate change.
questions.explanation: A) Harness(verb) – utilize, exploit, channel, employ, control प्रयोग करना Synonym: Utilize(verb) – Harness, employ, use (उपयोग करना) Waste(verb) – Squander, misuse, dissipate (बर्बाद करना) Neglect(verb) – Ignore, disregard, overlook (उपेक्षा करना) Ignore(verb) – Disregard, overlook, dismiss (नज़रअंदाज़ करना)

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word
questions.explanation: C) The correct spelling of the word Obedence is ‘Obedience’, which means “the quality of being obedient or submissive to authority” (आज्ञापालन या अनुशासन का गुण).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Tom will _________ the document to the office tomorrow.
questions.explanation: B)Mail'का use सही होगा क्योंकि "mail" का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को विशेष रूप से डाक या किसी अन्य माध्यम से भेजना। sentence में "Tom will _________ the document to the office tomorrow" का संदर्भ यह है कि वह दस्तावेज़ को कार्यालय भेजेगा, इसलिए 'mail' सबसे उपयुक्त विकल्प है। 'Meal' का अर्थ है भोजन, जो इस वाक्य में उपयुक्त नहीं है। 'Male' का अर्थ है पुरुष, जो यहाँ प्रसंग से मेल नहीं खाता। 'Mall' का अर्थ है एक शॉपिंग कॉम्प्लेक्स, जो इस संदर्भ में अर्थहीन है। 'Mail'will be used because it means to send something, especially through post or any other medium. In the sentence, "Tom will _________ the document to the office tomorrow," it implies sending the document, making 'mail' the correct choice. Whereas: 'Meal' means food, which doesn't fit the context. 'Male' means a man, which is irrelevant here. 'Mall' refers to a shopping complex, which doesn't make sense in this context.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The police announced a strict crackdown on illegal activities in the city.
questions.explanation: B) Crackdown(on) (noun) – clampdown, enforcement, suppression, repression, action सख़्त कार्रवाई Synonym: Suppression(noun) – Control, restriction, crackdown. (दमन) Tolerance(noun) – Acceptance, endurance, patience. (सहनशीलता) Encouragement(noun) – Support, motivation, incentive. (प्रोत्साहन) Freedom(noun) – Liberty, independence, autonomy. (स्वतंत्रता)

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word from the given sentence. The teacher's encouragment motivated the students to excel in their studies.
questions.explanation: A) The incorrectly spelt word in the sentence is "Encouragment", and the correct spelling is "Encouragement". प्रोत्साहन देना।

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A difficult situation in which you cannot make progress easily because there are too many things to do
questions.explanation: D)Logjam(noun) – A situation in which no progress can be made due to a number of difficulties or obstacles. गतिरोध

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The government took measures to shield small businesses from the economic crisis.
questions.explanation: A) Shield(verb) – protect, safeguard, defend, shelter, guard बचाना Synonym: Protect(verb) – Guard, secure. (सुरक्षित रखना) Expose(verb) – Reveal, uncover. (उजागर करना) Abandon(verb) – Desert, leave. (छोड़ देना) Ignore(verb) – Neglect, disregard. (अनदेखा करना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The new employee is really trying to fit in, but sometimes he feels out of place and uncomfortable.
questions.explanation: D)A fish out of water– Someone who feels out of place and uncomfortable (बेगानी जगह पर असहज महसूस करना) A bird of a feather– People with similar interests or characteristics समान विचारधारा वाले लोग A diamond in the rough– Someone with potential but needing improvement संभावनाओं से भरा लेकिन सुधार योग्य व्यक्ति A needle in a haystack– Something extremely difficult to find बहुत मुश्किल से मिलने वाली चीज़

questions.question: Improve the bold part in each of the following sentences, if needed. My mother is one of the few people to whom I look up to.
options: to which I look up to
questions.explanation: A) ‘to whom I look up to' के बदले ‘I look up to' या 'that I look up to' का प्रयोग होगा क्योकि 'look up to' एक Phrasal Verb है जिसका अर्थ है ‘प्रशंसा करना/आदर करना'। अतः 'to whom' का प्रयोग अनावश्यक है। 'whom' की जगह ‘That’ का प्रयोग हो सकता है क्योकि 'the few'/'the little' का प्रयोग होने पर इनके बाद Relative Pronoun के रूप में 'that' का प्रयोग होता है; जैसे - A) 'to whom I look up to' will be replaced with 'I look up to' or 'that I look up to' because 'look up to' is a Phrasal Verb which means 'to praise/respect' Doing'. Hence the use of 'to whom' is unnecessary. 'That' can be used instead of 'whom' because 'that' is used as a Relative Pronoun after 'the few'/'the little'; like -

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The unexpected guest at the party was a bull in a China shop.
questions.explanation: B)a bull in a China shop(idiom) – Careless and damaging असावधान और हानिकारक

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined phrase. Can you please stop continuously complaining about the student's shortcomings?
questions.explanation: C)Whining– Continuously complaining about (शिकायत करना)

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. People whom are (A) / concerned can avoid products (B) / that contain triclosan by (C) / reading the labels. (D)
questions.explanation: A) whom' के बदले 'who' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि यहाँ Relative Pronoun Subject के रूप में प्रयुक्त हुआ है (Verb 'are' के Subject के रूप में) अतः Nominative Case 'who' का प्रयोग होगा, Objective Case 'whom' का नहीं; जैसे— People who are interested can join the club. 'who' will be used instead of 'whom' because here the Relative Pronoun is used as the Subject (as the Subject of the verb 'are'), hence the Nominative Case 'who' will be used, not the Objective Case 'whom'; Like— People who are interested can join the club.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. There is a tendency among legislators to seek greener pastures (A) / at a time of crisis for their parties, but the nature of these defections (B) / suggest that this was not a simple case of leaving a party (C) / whose political strength had considerably diminished. (D)
options: There is a tendency among legislators to seek greener pastures
at a time of crisis for their parties, but the nature of these defections
suggest that this was not a simple case of leaving a party
whose political strength had considerably diminished.
questions.explanation: 'C) suggest' के बदले 'suggests' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि Subject 'the nature' Singular है अत: Verb भी Singular होगी; जैसे— The nature of these changes suggests a deeper problem. 'suggests' will be used instead of 'suggest' because the subject 'the nature' is singular, so the verb will also be singular; Like— The nature of these changes suggests a deeper problem.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph a) Worse, learning levels decreased moving from the third to the tenth grade b) The NAS 2021 survey assessed over 3.4 million students across grades 3, 5, 8, and 10, finding that c) The National Achievement Survey (NAS) 2021, a large-scale national survey conducted every three years by the National Council of Education Research and Training (NCERT), assesses the level of children’s learning d) The average score across all grades and subjects were lower in 2021 than they were in 2017
questions.explanation: D) c, b, d, a Option Astarts with d, which doesn't introduce the reader to NAS or the purpose of the survey, so A can be eliminated. Option Balso starts with d and does not introduce the acronym NAS, so B can be eliminated Option Cstarts correctly with c, but the flow between b and d is reversed, making the details disjointed. So, D can be eliminated Option D follows the logical sequence -c (introduction) -> b (details of the survey) -> d (findings) -> a (elaboration on the findings). Hence, it is the correct sequence. The "Worse," at the beginning of sentence a suggests that an addition or further elaboration of the negative trend indicated in sentence d

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Self-analysis is ______1____ for attaining success in one’s professional and spiritual lives, as it ____2______virtually all spiritual practices. Swami Sivananda laid great emphasis ______3______this aspect and prescribed it as an important chapter in his ‘20 Spiritual Instructions’. He advocated recording one’s daily sadhana in a spiritual diary and reviewing one’s progress periodically. Self-inspection, also known as ‘first party audit’, is an important part of any modern management system. Here, selected personnel of different departments, ___4____one department being audited, serve as auditors. Any nonconformance is recorded; root cause investigation ______5_____ made and appropriate corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) are initiated. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1
questions.explanation: C) 'Indispensable' का use होगा क्योंकि "indispensable" का अर्थ होता है अनिवार्य या अत्यावश्यक। passage के अनुसार, self-analysis को सफलता प्राप्त करने के लिए जरूरी माना गया है, जो कि व्यावसायिक और आध्यात्मिक जीवन दोनों में महत्वपूर्ण है। अतः 'indispensable' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Unpredictable' का अर्थ है अनिश्चित, 'Favorable' का अर्थ है अनुकूल, और 'Unseasonable' का अर्थ है असमय, जो कि इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'Indispensable' will be used because it means absolutely necessary or essential. According to the passage, self-analysis is deemed essential for achieving success, which is crucial in both professional and spiritual lives, making 'indispensable' fitting here. Whereas, 'Unpredictable' means not able to be predicted, 'Favorable' means advantageous, and 'Unseasonable' refers to something not in season or out of time, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Self-analysis is ______1____ for attaining success in one’s professional and spiritual lives, as it ____2______virtually all spiritual practices. Swami Sivananda laid great emphasis ______3______this aspect and prescribed it as an important chapter in his ‘20 Spiritual Instructions’. He advocated recording one’s daily sadhana in a spiritual diary and reviewing one’s progress periodically. Self-inspection, also known as ‘first party audit’, is an important part of any modern management system. Here, selected personnel of different departments, ___4____one department being audited, serve as auditors. Any nonconformance is recorded; root cause investigation ______5_____ made and appropriate corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) are initiated. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: A) 'Touches upon' का use होगा क्योंकि "touches upon" का अर्थ होता है किसी विषय पर छूना या संक्षेप में चर्चा करना। passage में mention है कि self-analysis लगभग सभी आध्यात्मिक प्रथाओं को प्रभावित करता है, जिससे 'touches upon' सही विकल्प बनता है क्योंकि यह उस प्रभाव को दिखाता है जो self-analysis इन प्रथाओं पर डालता है। जबकि 'Count on' का अर्थ है भरोसा करना, 'Commence upon' का अर्थ है शुरुआत करना पर किसी चीज पर, और 'Lights on' का उपयोग आमतौर पर प्रकाश डालने के लिहाज से नहीं होता है, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं है। 'Touches upon' will be used because it means to mention or deal with a subject in a brief or slight way. The passage states that self-analysis impacts virtually all spiritual practices, making 'touches upon' the appropriate choice as it reflects the influence of self-analysis on these practices. Whereas, 'Count on' means to rely on someone or something, 'Commence upon' means to begin something on a specific subject, and 'Lights on' is not commonly used to mean shed light on in a figurative sense, which don't fit in this context

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Self-analysis is ______1____ for attaining success in one’s professional and spiritual lives, as it ____2______virtually all spiritual practices. Swami Sivananda laid great emphasis ______3______this aspect and prescribed it as an important chapter in his ‘20 Spiritual Instructions’. He advocated recording one’s daily sadhana in a spiritual diary and reviewing one’s progress periodically. Self-inspection, also known as ‘first party audit’, is an important part of any modern management system. Here, selected personnel of different departments, ___4____one department being audited, serve as auditors. Any nonconformance is recorded; root cause investigation ______5_____ made and appropriate corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) are initiated. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: D) On' का use होगा क्योंकि "on" इस संदर्भ में एक विशेष विषय या बिंदु पर ध्यान केंद्रित करने का इशारा करता है। passage में mention है कि Swami Sivananda ने इस पहलू पर महत्वपूर्ण जोर दिया, जिससे 'on' यहाँ उपयुक्त option है, क्योंकि यह उस विशिष्ट बिंदु पर ध्यान केंद्रित करने के संदर्भ को सही तरीके से दर्शाता है। 'By' का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज के द्वारा, 'For' का अर्थ होता है किसी उद्देश्य के लिए, और 'In' का उपयोग स्थानिक संदर्भ में अधिक होता है, जो इस संदर्भ में उपयुक्त नहीं हैं। 'On' will be used because it indicates focusing on a specific subject or point. The passage mentions that Swami Sivananda laid great emphasis on this aspect, making 'on' the appropriate choice here as it correctly represents the focus on that particular point. 'By' indicates a method or means, 'For' is used for the purpose of something, and 'In' is typically used for spatial contexts, which are not suitable here.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Self-analysis is ______1____ for attaining success in one’s professional and spiritual lives, as it ____2______virtually all spiritual practices. Swami Sivananda laid great emphasis ______3______this aspect and prescribed it as an important chapter in his ‘20 Spiritual Instructions’. He advocated recording one’s daily sadhana in a spiritual diary and reviewing one’s progress periodically. Self-inspection, also known as ‘first party audit’, is an important part of any modern management system. Here, selected personnel of different departments, ___4____one department being audited, serve as auditors. Any nonconformance is recorded; root cause investigation ______5_____ made and appropriate corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) are initiated. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: B) 'Barring' का use होगा क्योंकि "barring" का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज या व्यक्ति को छोड़कर। passage में mention है कि चुने हुए कर्मचारी विभिन्न विभागों से, जिनमें से एक विभाग का ऑडिट किया जा रहा है, ऑडिटर के रूप में कार्य करते हैं। इसलिए 'barring' यहाँ सही option है, जो इंगित करता है कि जिस विभाग का ऑडिट किया जा रहा है, उसे छोड़कर अन्य विभाग के लोग ऑडिटर के रूप में सेवा करते हैं। 'Accept' का अर्थ होता है स्वीकार करना, 'Endure' का अर्थ है सहन करना, और 'Taking' का अर्थ होता है लेना या ग्रहण करना, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। 'Barring' will be used because it means excluding. The passage states that selected personnel from different departments, except for the one being audited, serve as auditors. Therefore, 'barring' is the correct choice here, indicating that personnel from departments other than the one being audited are involved. 'Accept' means to agree to receive, 'Endure' means to suffer something painful or difficult patiently, and 'Taking' means to begin to hold or use, which are not appropriate in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Self-analysis is ______1____ for attaining success in one’s professional and spiritual lives, as it ____2______virtually all spiritual practices. Swami Sivananda laid great emphasis ______3______this aspect and prescribed it as an important chapter in his ‘20 Spiritual Instructions’. He advocated recording one’s daily sadhana in a spiritual diary and reviewing one’s progress periodically. Self-inspection, also known as ‘first party audit’, is an important part of any modern management system. Here, selected personnel of different departments, ___4____one department being audited, serve as auditors. Any nonconformance is recorded; root cause investigation ______5_____ made and appropriate corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) are initiated. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: A) 'Is' का use होगा क्योंकि यहाँ पर "root cause investigation" एक व्यक्ति या घटना के बजाय एक process का वर्णन करता है, जो एकवचन (singular) है। इसलिए, 'is' इस के लिए सही verb है। 'Are' का use बहुवचन (plural) के लिए होता है, और 'was' और 'were' past tense के लिए होते हैं, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। 'Is' will be used because "root cause investigation" describes a process rather than a person or event, and is singular. Therefore, 'is' is the appropriate verb for this process. 'Are' is used for plural subjects, and 'was' and 'were' are past tense, which are not suitable here as the sentence describes a process that is currently ongoing.
